quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Newton's Law of Cooling"?

options.text: A relation where y is defined implicitly by an equation F(x,y) = 0 rather than explicitly as y = f(x)
First derivative test: always works but requires sign analysis on an interval; second derivative test: simpler but can be inconclusive
dT/dt = -k(T - Tₛ); object's temperature changes at a rate proportional to the difference from ambient temperature Tₛ
Product rule: two functions multiplied; Quotient rule: division; Chain rule: composition (function inside another function)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Newton's Law of Cooling" is defined as: dT/dt = -k(T - Tₛ); object's temperature changes at a rate proportional to the difference from ambient temperature Tₛ. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Mixing problems"?

options.text: Sometimes algebraic simplification (canceling, expanding, dividing) before differentiation is easier than applying rules to complex expressions
Priority for choosing u: Logarithmic > Inverse trig > Algebraic > Trigonometric > Exponential; u is differentiated, dv is integrated
If f'(x) = 0 for all x in an interval, MVT proves f must be a constant function on that interval
dQ/dt = (concentration_in × flow_in) - (Q/V × flow_out); tracks the amount of substance Q in a tank with inflow and outflow

quiz_questions.explanation: "Mixing problems" is defined as: dQ/dt = (concentration_in × flow_in) - (Q/V × flow_out); tracks the amount of substance Q in a tank with inflow and outflow. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Drug metabolism"?

options.text: At a critical point c: f' changes + to -: local max; f' changes - to +: local min; no sign change: neither
f''(x) > 0; the graph curves upward like a cup; the slope is increasing
dC/dt = -kC + D(t); drug concentration C decays exponentially while dosing function D(t) adds periodic inputs
For definite integrals: convert limits from x to u (u_lower = g(a), u_upper = g(b)); no need to substitute back

quiz_questions.explanation: "Drug metabolism" is defined as: dC/dt = -kC + D(t); drug concentration C decays exponentially while dosing function D(t) adds periodic inputs. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Setting up a DE from a word problem"?

options.text: Identify the changing quantity, determine what the rate of change depends on, write the equation, specify initial conditions
MVT + IVT: first use MVT to show f' achieves certain values, then use IVT on f' to show intermediate values exist
Numerical approximation: start at (x₀,y₀), step by yₙ₊₁ = yₙ + f(xₙ,yₙ)·Δx with step size Δx
When v(t) = 0 AND v(t) changes sign; the particle reverses its direction of travel

quiz_questions.explanation: "Setting up a DE from a word problem" is defined as: Identify the changing quantity, determine what the rate of change depends on, write the equation, specify initial conditions. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Interpreting solutions"?

options.text: First derivative test: always works but requires sign analysis on an interval; second derivative test: simpler but can be inconclusive
The mathematical solution must be translated back to the physical context: what does the asymptotic behavior mean? When does the quantity reach a target?
Reading the y-value a function approaches by tracing the curve from both sides toward x = c; the function need not be defined at c
P = K: if the population is perturbed slightly above or below K, it returns to K

quiz_questions.explanation: "Interpreting solutions" is defined as: The mathematical solution must be translated back to the physical context: what does the asymptotic behavior mean? When does the quantity reach a target?. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "dT/dt = -k(T - Tₛ); object's temperature changes at a rate proportional to the difference from ambient temperature Tₛ"?

options.text: Newton's Law of Cooling
Tangent lines to implicit curves
When does the particle change direction?
Total vs. net

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Newton's Law of Cooling".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "dQ/dt = (concentration_in × flow_in) - (Q/V × flow_out); tracks the amount of substance Q in a tank with inflow and outflow"?

options.text: Epsilon (ε)
Error in approximation
Multiple chain rule
Mixing problems

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "dC/dt = -kC + D(t); drug concentration C decays exponentially while dosing function D(t) adds periodic inputs"?

options.text: Free response strategy
Multiple chain rule
Cumulative distribution function (CDF)
Drug metabolism

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Identify the changing quantity, determine what the rate of change depends on, write the equation, specify initial conditions"?

options.text: When does the particle change direction?
Cardiac output
Setting up a DE from a word problem
Tangent lines to implicit curves

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Setting up a DE from a word problem".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The mathematical solution must be translated back to the physical context: what does the asymptotic behavior mean? When does the quantity reach a target?"?

options.text: Interpreting solutions
Common quotient rule errors
Domain restrictions
Epsilon (ε)

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations is accurate?

options.text: Implicit Differentiation finds dy/dx when y is not explicitly solved as a function of x.
These formulas arise from the Pythagorean theorem applied to infinitesimal arc segments: ds = √(dx² + dy²) = √(1 + (dy/dx)²)dx.
Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations applies DEs to diverse practical scenarios.
Success requires clear justification of setup, correct antiderivatives, appropriate use of technology, and precise interpretation of results in the given context.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations?

options.text: Differential Equation Basics introduces equations involving derivatives.
These formulas arise from the Pythagorean theorem applied to infinitesimal arc segments: ds = √(dx² + dy²) = √(1 + (dy/dx)²)dx.
Limits and Continuity are the foundation of calculus.
Newton's Law of Cooling: dT/dt = -k(T - Tₛ) models temperature equilibration.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations?

options.text: The theorem fails if the interval is open (extrema may not be attained), the function is not continuous (could have gaps), or the domain is unbounded.
Continuous compound interest follows A = Pe^(rt).
Choose u and dv from the integrand using the LIATE priority: Logarithmic, Inverse trig, Algebraic, Trigonometric, Exponential (choose u from earlier in the list).
Mixing problems: dC/dt = (rate in) - (rate out) models pollutant concentration in a tank.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations?

options.text: The product rule extends to three or more factors: d/dx[fgh] = f'gh + fg'h + fgh'.
This technique is essential for functions like xˣ, (sin x)^x, or x^(1/x) where both base and exponent are variable.
Drug dosing models use DEs to track drug concentration in the bloodstream.
Visualizing MVT helps interpret derivative graphs and connect graphical and analytical representations.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations?

options.text: The power rule: d/dx[xⁿ] = nxⁿ⁻¹ for any real n.
Each model requires translating physical principles into a DE, solving it, and interpreting the solution in context.
Success requires clear justification of setup, correct antiderivatives, appropriate use of technology, and precise interpretation of results in the given context.

The method: (1) identify the quantity to optimize, (2) express it as a function of one variable using constraints, (3) find critical points where f'(x) = 0, (4) verify maxima/minima using the first or second derivative test, (5) check endpoints...

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations?

options.text: AP Calculus free-response questions consistently test the ability to set up integrals from verbal descriptions and geometric reasoning
Forensic science: Newton's Law of Cooling estimates time of death from a body's cooling rate relative to ambient temperature
Temperature modeling: since temperature changes continuously, the IVT guarantees that between any two measured temperatures, every intermediate value was achieved
Proving that a polynomial of degree n has at most n-1 critical points: applying Rolle's Theorem between consecutive roots of f

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations?

options.text: Market saturation: adoption of new technology (smartphones, streaming services) follows an S-curve as the product approaches market saturation
Radiocarbon dating: ¹⁴C decays with t₁/₂ ≈ 5,730 years; measuring remaining ¹⁴C in organic material determines its age up to ~50,000 years
Population modeling: if the growth rate dP/dt = f(t) is known, integrating gives the population P(t) = ∫f(t)dt + P₀
Environmental engineering: mixing models predict pollutant concentration in lakes and rivers, informing cleanup timelines and regulations

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Average temperature: f_avg = (1/24)∫₀²⁴ T(t)dt gives the average temperature over a 24-hour period, used for energy consumption estimates
Radioactive decay: integrating the decay rate dN/dt = -λN gives N(t) = N₀e^(-λt), the fundamental decay equation
Pharmacology: drug concentration models determine optimal dosing intervals to maintain therapeutic levels while avoiding toxic accumulation
Traffic analysis: given a graph of vehicle entry rate, computing total vehicles and identifying peak congestion periods

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations?

options.text: Forensic science: Newton's Law of Cooling estimates time of death from a body's cooling rate relative to ambient temperature
Finding the slope of a circle x² + y² = 25 at any point: dy/dx = -x/y, giving the tangent line direction for navigation and engineering applications
Financial analysis: if an investment grows from $1000 to $1200 over a year, MVT guarantees the instantaneous growth rate was exactly 20% at some moment
Dam design: engineers compute total hydrostatic force on dam faces to determine required structural strength and materials

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations in practice?

options.text: Computer algebra systems: software like Wolfram Alpha uses algorithmic decision trees similar to this strategy to compute symbolic integrals automatically
Temperature modeling: since temperature changes continuously, the IVT guarantees that between any two measured temperatures, every intermediate value was achieved
Environmental engineering: mixing models predict pollutant concentration in lakes and rivers, informing cleanup timelines and regulations
Traffic analysis: given a graph of vehicle entry rate, computing total vehicles and identifying peak congestion periods

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Real-World Modeling with Differential Equations.
